checks.name: Re-run live aging-pipeline repro against 30f36e0
checks.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Re-seeded the EXACT scenario from the prior review pass (3 applications at status='interview', 1 at 'offer', 0 at 'screening') via a temporary scratch pytest against the live fix branch. Result: stages={'jobs_found':4,'applied':4,'screened':4,'interviewed':4,'offers':1}, dropoffs=[{'after':'jobs_found','count':0},{'after':'applied','count':0},{'after':'screened','count':0},{'after':'interviewed','count':3}] — every dropoff >= 0, the -3 is gone. Scratch test file removed after the run via restoring from a pre-probe backup; worktree verified clean (git status --short empty) afterward. Also confirmed via the new frontend test (see check (c) below) that SankeyFlow.tsx no longer emits '−-N' for any negative input, thanks to the Math.max(0, ...) clamp at line ~114.

checks.name: Cumulative stage definitions correct/monotonic, match funnel()'s IN-clause semantics
checks.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Diffed apps/api/app/routers/applications.py funnel_sankey()'s new query character-for-character against the unchanged analytics.py funnel() query: both now use identical `COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE "status" IN ('screening','interview','offer')) AS screened`, `... IN ('interview','offer') AS interviewed`, `... = 'offer' AS offers`. 'Applied' now comes from `get_application_counts(cur, uid)['submitted']` (no period_clause passed, i.e. all-time — correct since funnel_sankey has no period param), which is the same canonical non-draft count analytics.funnel() uses at period='all'. Because each stage's IN-list is a superset of the next (offer ⊆ {interview,offer} ⊆ {screening,interview,offer} ⊆ all non-draft statuses), every dropoff is guaranteed >= 0 by construction — proved both mathematically and live (check a).

checks.name: New pytest genuinely fails on be7b240
checks.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Transplanted test_aging_pipeline_never_produces_a_negative_dropoff verbatim onto be7b240 in a disposable worktree and ran it: FAILED with `assert 0 == 4` on the `values['applied'] == 4` assertion (be7b240's stage-exclusive funnel_sankey() found 0 applications with literal status=='submitted', since all 4 seeded apps were at 'interview'/'offer') — matches the author's exact claimed fail-before result. Ran on 30f36e0: passes. Disposable worktree removed after. Also transplanted the new frontend apps/web/src/components/applications/__tests__/SankeyFlow.test.tsx onto be7b240 (node_modules symlinked from the fix-branch worktree): FAILED with the received text literally containing '−-3 · no response / screened out' — proving the frontend clamp test is genuine, not tautological. Disposable worktree removed after.

checks.name: Both MV-application-tracker-005 (undercount) and MV-application-tracker-006 (negative-dropoff render) resolved
checks.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] tracker-005 (Sankey 'Applied' diverging from canonical submitted count): resolved — 'Applied' now derives from get_application_counts(), and the new test asserts `sankey['stages'][1]['value'] == funnel['applied'] == 4`, i.e. the two now agree by construction, live-verified. analytics.py's get_application_counts() docstring (lines ~44-60) no longer excludes funnel_sankey() from its 'single source of truth' claim — it now correctly states 'funnel_sankey() now also calls this function for its Applied node — every cumulative surface derives from this one function, with no divergent queries left,' which is accurate given the diff. tracker-006 (negative-dropoff broken render): resolved at both layers — backend query is now monotonic (proved in check b), and the frontend has a defense-in-depth Math.max(0, f.dropoff.count) clamp (SankeyFlow.tsx) plus a dedicated regression test that feeds a deliberately negative dropoff and asserts the render clamps to '−0' instead of '−-3'. Both the root cause (backend model) and a defensive belt-and-suspenders guard (frontend clamp) are in place — this is good defensive engineering, not just a patch at one layer.

checks.name: Issue-1 tooltip/header still intact
checks.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] `git diff be7b240..30f36e0 --name-only` does not touch apps/web/src/app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx or DashboardStats.tsx at all. Directly read apps/web/src/app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx at 30f36e0: line 95 still reads 'Every application record created in the selected period — draft through offer or rejection.' and line 150 still renders 'Dashboard summary ({period})' — unchanged from the already-PASSED be7b240 state.

positive_findings_for_the_record: The rework correctly implements Path 2 (cumulative model) exactly as the review evidence indicated it should, rather than defending the disproven Path 1 further.

The author's own new test directly reproduces the reviewer's exact adversarial repro scenario (3x interview + 1x offer + 0x screening) as a permanent regression test, and also removed the now-inaccurate MV-application-tracker-005 pinning test and docstring claims rather than leaving stale/contradictory documentation.
Defense-in-depth: both the root-cause backend fix AND a frontend render-side clamp were added, with dedicated tests for each layer.
All independently-reproduced fail-before/pass-after checks (2 backend, 1 frontend) confirm genuine, non-tautological tests.
No regressions across three passes of this review: issue-1's fix (from pass 2) remains completely untouched and intact in this delta.
